source_pr.title: binloginfo: fix a data race on global.Config() in the unit test
source_pr.body:
```
WARNING: DATA RACE
Read at 0x000002591538 by goroutine 20:
  github.com/pingcap/tidb/sessionctx/binloginfo.(*BinlogInfo).WriteBinlog()
      /home/jenkins/workspace/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/go/src/github.com/pingcap/tidb/sessionctx/binloginfo/binloginfo.go:118 +0x667
  github.com/pingcap/tidb/store/tikv.(*twoPhaseCommitter).writeFinishBinlog.func1()
      /home/jenkins/workspace/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/go/src/github.com/pingcap/tidb/store/tikv/2pc.go:683 +0x6b

Previous write at 0x000002591538 by goroutine 53:
  github.com/pingcap/tidb/sessionctx/binloginfo_test.(*testBinlogSuite).TestIgnoreError()
      /home/jenkins/workspace/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/go/src/github.com/pingcap/tidb/sessionctx/binloginfo/binloginfo_test.go:395 +0x499
  github.com/pingcap/tidb/util/testkit.(*TestKit).Exec()
      /home/jenkins/workspace/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/go/src/github.com/pingcap/tidb/util/testkit/testkit.go:130 +0x133
  github.com/pingcap/tidb/util/testkit.(*TestKit).MustExec()
      /home/jenkins/workspace/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/go/src/github.com/pingcap/tidb/util/testkit/testkit.go:159 +0x91
  github.com/pingcap/tidb/sessionctx/binloginfo_test.(*testBinlogSuite).TestIgnoreError()
      /home/jenkins/workspace/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/go/src/github.com/pingcap/tidb/sessionctx/binloginfo/binloginfo_test.go:378 +0x275
  github.com/pingcap/tidb/sessionctx/binloginfo_test.(*testBinlogSuite).TestIgnoreError()
```

I should make a atomic variable, rather than operate on config.Binlog directly.

```
goroutine1:
       	cfg := config.GetGlobalConfig()
	if cfg.Binlog.IgnoreError {

goroutine2 (in test code):

	cfg := config.GetGlobalConfig()
	cfg.Binlog.IgnoreError = true
```

I'll update https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/6546 when this is merged.

@coocood @shenli

symptoms: binloginfo: fix a data race on global.Config() in the unit test
failure_signature: binloginfo: fix a data race on global.Config() in the unit test
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to a data race on shared global configuration: the unit test mutated `global.Config().Binlog.IgnoreError` while binlog writing could concurrently read it in another goroutine. The patch stabilizes it by decoupling runtime behavior from direct global-config reads (using atomic flags / explicit setters) so tests no longer mutate shared global config concurrently.
fix_pattern: Avoid concurrent reads/writes of global config; use atomic flags or local copies configured once, and keep tests from mutating shared globals during concurrent execution.

evidence: evidence: binloginfo: fix a data race on global.Config() in the unit test
